notes: Level 3 manual review override shard for spell indexes 0-19 in generated discovery row order.

animate-dead::choice_or_mode.issue: Animate Dead chooses between bones and corpse, which determines Skeleton versus Zombie output; runtime only stores this in prose.
animate-dead::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add source-material mode fields for bones_to_skeleton and corpse_to_zombie.
animate-dead::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime summon/sourceMode data for Skeleton versus Zombie creation.

animate-dead::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The detector matched command wording, but Animate Dead has no attack roll, save, or check modifier in the cited sentence.
animate-dead::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Track command mechanics under summon/control and sustain action fields.
animate-dead::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Track command mechanics under summon/control data.

animate-dead::multi_instance_or_split_ta.issue: Reasserting control can affect up to four existing creatures, and higher-level slots add two more Undead per slot; runtime has no typed count/scaling data.
animate-dead::multi_instance_or_split_ta.recommendedTemplateChange: Add animate/reassert count fields for base reassert count and +2 creatures per slot above 3.
animate-dead::multi_instance_or_split_ta.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime target/count scaling for animate or reassert control modes.
animate-dead::multi_instance_or_split_ta.closedReason: Closed the reassert-control count by adding `controlled_undead` to the target-instance vocabulary and recording Animate Dead's reassert mode as up to four existing undead, plus two per slot level above 3, in structured markdown and runtime targeting.instanceAllocation. The separate animate-versus-reassert mode, source corpse/bones eligibility, undead output, command action, control duration, and transformation mechanics remain open under other Animate Dead rows.

animate-dead::target_filter_or_eligibili.issue: The spell targets a pile of bones or corpse of a Medium or Small Humanoid; runtime was corrected from creatures to objects but cannot express corpse/bones, Humanoid origin, or size.
animate-dead::target_filter_or_eligibili.recommendedTemplateChange: Add corpse/remains eligibility fields for source type, original creature type, and size.
animate-dead::target_filter_or_eligibili.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime object/remains filters for bones/corpse, Medium or Small, Humanoid.

animate-dead::sustain_or_recast_action.issue: A Bonus Action can mentally command controlled creatures within 60 feet, and recasting before 24 hours reasserts control; runtime only stores this as prose.
animate-dead::sustain_or_recast_action.recommendedTemplateChange: Add controlled-summon command action and recast-to-maintain-control fields.
animate-dead::sustain_or_recast_action.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime commandAction and reassertControl data.

animate-dead::summon_or_controlled_entit.issue: The spell creates Skeleton or Zombie servants, controls them for 24 hours, supports shared commands, default Dodge behavior, and task persistence; runtime has only a generic utility description.
animate-dead::summon_or_controlled_entit.recommendedTemplateChange: Add undead summon/control fields for creature output, control duration, command behavior, default action, and task continuation.
animate-dead::summon_or_controlled_entit.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime summonEntity/control data for animated undead.

animate-dead::status_or_state_change.issue: The target remains become an Undead Skeleton or Zombie; runtime lacks a first-class transformation/animation state.
animate-dead::status_or_state_change.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime transformation data from corpse/bones to Undead creature.

aura-of-vitality::object_stats_or_damage.issue: The detector matched Hit Points wording, but Aura of Vitality is healing, not object stats or damageability; runtime now uses HEALING effects.
aura-of-vitality::object_stats_or_damage.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by replacing prose utility with immediate and turn-start HEALING effects.

beacon-of-hope::choice_or_mode.issue: Beacon of Hope can choose any number of creatures, but runtime targeting uses maxTargets 1.
beacon-of-hope::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime target count data for any number of creatures within range.

beacon-of-hope::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The spell grants Advantage on Wisdom saving throws and Death Saving Throws, but runtime stores this in prose only.
beacon-of-hope::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add saving throw advantage fields scoped by save category.
beacon-of-hope::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime saveAdvantage data for Wisdom saves and Death Saving Throws.

beacon-of-hope::healing_or_restoration.issue: Targets regain the maximum possible Hit Points from any healing, but runtime has no typed healing maximization modifier.
beacon-of-hope::healing_or_restoration.recommendedTemplateChange: Add healing modifier fields for maximize incoming healing.
beacon-of-hope::healing_or_restoration.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime healingModifier data for maximum healing received.

beacon-of-hope::object_stats_or_damageab.issue: The detector matched Hit Points wording, but Beacon of Hope modifies healing received rather than object stats or damageability.

bestow-curse::choice_or_mode.issue: Bestow Curse has multiple curse options and an open-ended GM-approved option; runtime stores all options in one utility description.
bestow-curse::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add curse option fields for ability-score disadvantage, attacks-against-caster disadvantage, action-waste save, extra necrotic damage, and GM-approved alternative.
bestow-curse::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime curseOptions data instead of a single utility description.

bestow-curse::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The initial Wisdom save is now represented, but the selected curse options include multiple d20 modifiers and a recurring Wisdom save that remain prose-only.
bestow-curse::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add option-scoped attack/save/check modifier fields and recurring save fields.
bestow-curse::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime curse option data for selected ability disadvantage, attacks against caster, and start-turn Wisdom save.

blinding-smite::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: Blinding Smite's damage and Blinded rider are hit-gated, and the repeat save is Constitution at turn end; runtime now reflects those mechanics.
blinding-smite::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional attack/save field required for this reviewed row.
blinding-smite::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing damage/status conditions to hit-gated and correcting repeatSave to Constitution.

blinding-smite::repeat_save_or_recurring.issue: The end-of-turn Constitution save that ends the spell on success is represented on the Blinded status payload.
blinding-smite::repeat_save_or_recurring.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by correcting repeatSave.saveType to Constitution.

blinding-smite::vision_light_sound.issue: The Blinded condition is represented as a STATUS_CONDITION effect, and the spell has no separate light/sound payload beyond Radiant damage presentation.
blinding-smite::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: No vision/light/sound field required beyond status condition data.
blinding-smite::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: No additional vision/light/sound JSON change required.

blinding-smite::status_or_state_change.issue: The Blinded condition is represented and now correctly hit-gated.
blinding-smite::status_or_state_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by marking structured Effect Type as DAMAGE, STATUS_CONDITION.
blinding-smite::status_or_state_change.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by preserving Blinded and correcting its gating and repeat save.

blinding-smite::conditional_ending.issue: The Blinded condition lasts until the spell ends, with the special end condition handled by the represented repeat save.
blinding-smite::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional conditional-ending field required for this reviewed row.
blinding-smite::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: No additional conditional-ending JSON change required.

blink::choice_or_mode.issue: Blink lets the caster choose the return space within 10 feet, with fallback to the nearest unoccupied space; runtime stores this in prose only.
blink::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add return-placement choice fields for visible unoccupied space within range and fallback nearest space.
blink::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime planarReturn data for chosen return location and fallback placement.

blink::target_filter_or_eligibility.issue: The return requires a visible unoccupied space within 10 feet or nearest unoccupied fallback; no structured/runtime placement filter exists.
blink::target_filter_or_eligibility.recommendedTemplateChange: Add unoccupied visible destination eligibility fields.

blink::vision_light_sound.issue: While on the Ethereal Plane, the caster can perceive the previous plane in gray out to 60 feet; this sensory limit is prose-only.
blink::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add planar perception fields for source plane, visual presentation, and range limit.
blink::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime planarPerception data for 60-foot gray perception.

blink::sensor_or_remote_perception.issue: Blink changes cross-plane perception and whether creatures on the other plane can perceive the caster; runtime has no typed planar perception/visibility barrier.
blink::sensor_or_remote_perception.recommendedTemplateChange: Add planar perception and cross-plane detectability fields.
blink::sensor_or_remote_perception.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime planarVisibility data for perceiving and being perceived across planes.

blink::travel_or_planar_movement.issue: Blink uses an end-of-turn 1d6 roll, shifts the caster to the Ethereal Plane on 4-6, ends instantly if already there, and returns the caster at start of next turn; runtime only has corrected prose.
blink::travel_or_planar_movement.recommendedTemplateChange: Add recurring planar shift fields for die roll, success range, destination plane, already-on-plane ending, return timing, and return placement.
blink::travel_or_planar_movement.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime planarShift data instead of prose-only utility text.

blink::conditional_ending.issue: The spell ends instantly if the caster is already on the Ethereal Plane when the shift would occur; this conditional ending is prose-only.
blink::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime ending condition for already being on the Ethereal Plane.
blink::conditional_ending.closedReason: Closed by adding the already_on_destination_plane conditional-ending trigger and recording Blink's Ethereal Plane self-ending condition in structured markdown and runtime JSON.

blink::aftermath_or_memory.issue: Returning when the spell ends is part of Blink's planar movement/return mechanic, not a separate aftermath or memory effect.

blink::deferred_descriptive_flavor.issue: The detector's flavor row includes mechanical planar movement, perception, and cross-plane interaction rules; pure presentation is not being deferred separately here.
blink::deferred_descriptive_flavor.recommendedTemplateChange: Resolve through planar movement and perception fields.
blink::deferred_descriptive_flavor.recommendedJsonChange: Resolve through planarShift and planarPerception data.

call-lightning::choice_or_mode.issue: The caster chooses a point under the cloud on cast and can later target the same or a different point; runtime lacks cloud-relative point targeting.
call-lightning::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime strikePoint data constrained to under the cloud.

call-lightning::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The Dexterity save for half Lightning damage is represented on the damage effect.
call-lightning::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: No attack/save field change required for this reviewed row.

call-lightning::vision_light_sound.issue: The spell creates or controls a visible storm cloud above the caster, but runtime only represents a generic area and damage effect.
call-lightning::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add visible cloud/object fields for storm cloud placement, cylinder dimensions, and above-caster constraint.
call-lightning::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime stormCloud data with cloud dimensions and position constraints.

call-lightning::environmental_change.issue: Outdoors in an existing storm, the spell controls that storm instead of creating a cloud and gains +1d10 damage; this environmental branch is prose-only.
call-lightning::environmental_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Add environmental branch fields for existing storm control and storm damage bonus.
call-lightning::environmental_change.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime environmentalCondition data for outdoors-in-storm mode and +1d10 damage.

call-lightning::conditional_ending.issue: The cited 'until the spell ends' text describes repeat use duration, not a special ending condition.
call-lightning::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Track repeat Magic action under sustain/recast fields.
call-lightning::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Track repeat strike under sustainAction data.

catnap::choice_or_mode.issue: Choosing up to three targets is ordinary multi-target selection and is now represented by Targeting Max 3.
catnap::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: No choice/mode field required beyond targeting.
catnap::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing runtime targeting to multi with maxTargets 3 and creatures only.

catnap::vision_light_sound.issue: The calming gesture is somatic presentation, and the can-see target requirement is represented by lineOfSight true.
catnap::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: No vision/light/sound field required for this reviewed row.

catnap::status_or_state_change.issue: Unconscious is now represented, but gaining a short rest after the full duration and immunity to Catnap until a long rest are still prose-only.
catnap::status_or_state_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Add rest-benefit and temporary recast-immunity fields tied to completing the full duration.
catnap::status_or_state_change.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime restBenefit and affectedBySpellLockout data.

catnap::conditional_ending.closedReason: Catnap now records target_takes_damage and adjacent_creature_action_shakes_awake as structured Condition Break Triggers and runtime statusCondition.breakTriggers. The full-duration short-rest benefit and long-rest lockout remain open under status_or_state_change.
catnap::conditional_ending.issue: The spell ends early on a target if it takes damage or someone uses an action to shake or slap it awake; these per-target ending conditions are prose-only.
catnap::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add per-target ending fields for damage and adjacent/action wake-up.
catnap::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime ending conditions for damage and action wake/slap.

clairvoyance::choice_or_mode.issue: The caster chooses seeing or hearing and can switch senses later; runtime stores this mode choice in prose only.
clairvoyance::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add remote sensor sense mode fields and active mode switching.
clairvoyance::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime sensorModes data for seeing/hearing and switch action.

clairvoyance::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The detector matched Bonus Action text, but Clairvoyance has no attack/save/check modifier.
clairvoyance::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Track the Bonus Action under sensor mode switching.
clairvoyance::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Track the Bonus Action under sensorModeSwitch data.

clairvoyance::sustain_or_recast_action.issue: Switching between seeing and hearing is a Bonus Action; runtime prose was corrected, but no typed action field exists.
clairvoyance::sustain_or_recast_action.recommendedTemplateChange: Add sensor mode switch action fields.

clairvoyance::vision_light_sound.issue: The sensor is Invisible but appears as a luminous fist-sized orb to creatures that can see it; seeing/hearing modes are also not typed.
clairvoyance::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add sensor visibility fields and sensory mode fields.
clairvoyance::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime sensor visibility and mode data.

clairvoyance::sensor_or_remote_perceptio.issue: Clairvoyance creates an intangible, invulnerable, stationary sensor in a familiar or obvious location and routes seeing or hearing through it; runtime has this only as utility prose.
clairvoyance::sensor_or_remote_perceptio.recommendedTemplateChange: Add remote sensor fields for location eligibility, invisibility, intangibility, invulnerability, stationary behavior, and sense routing.

clairvoyance::status_or_state_change.issue: Invisible describes the sensor's visibility property, not an Invisible condition applied to a creature.

conjure-animals::choice_or_mode.issue: The chosen animal form is cosmetic in the 2024 pack version because the pack keeps the same mechanics regardless of wolves, serpents, birds, or another form.
conjure-animals::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: No mechanical mode field required for animal appearance in this goal.
conjure-animals::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: No runtime mechanical field required for animal appearance in this goal.

conjure-animals::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The caster gains Advantage on Strength saves near the pack, and creatures can be forced to make Dexterity saves from several once-per-turn pack proximity triggers; runtime lacks both the save advantage and the damage trigger model.
conjure-animals::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add aura-adjacent save advantage fields and pack proximity damage trigger fields.
conjure-animals::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime saveAdvantage and packDamageTrigger data.

conjure-animals::target_filter_or_eligib.issue: The pack appears and moves to unoccupied visible spaces; runtime has no unoccupied-space placement filter for the pack.
conjure-animals::target_filter_or_eligib.recommendedTemplateChange: Add unoccupied visible space fields for conjured packs.
conjure-animals::target_filter_or_eligib.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime placement filters for pack creation and movement.

conjure-animals::vision_light_sound.issue: The detector matched visibility requirements and spectral appearance; mechanical visibility requirements are target/placement filters, while spectral appearance is deferred flavor.
conjure-animals::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: No separate vision/light/sound JSON change required.

conjure-animals::summon_or_controlled_en.issue: The spell creates a Large spectral intangible pack that lasts for the duration and moves when the caster moves; runtime still contains older summon-style prose and lacks 2024 pack data.
conjure-animals::summon_or_controlled_en.recommendedTemplateChange: Add conjured pack fields for size, intangibility, movement coupling, threat radius, and duration.
conjure-animals::summon_or_controlled_en.recommendedJsonChange: Replace old summon creature prose with runtime conjuredPack data.

conjure-animals::deferred_descriptive_fl.issue: The specific animal appearance examples are cosmetic once Large spectral intangible pack mechanics are tracked separately.
conjure-animals::deferred_descriptive_fl.recommendedTemplateChange: No field required for the exact animal appearance examples.
conjure-animals::deferred_descriptive_fl.recommendedJsonChange: No runtime field required for the exact animal appearance examples.

conjure-barrage::choice_or_mode.issue: The spell affects each creature of the caster's choice in the cone, meaning the caster can exclude creatures in the area; runtime has no area-exclusion choice model.
conjure-barrage::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add area target-selection fields for chosen creatures in an area.
conjure-barrage::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime selectableAreaTargets data so the caster can choose affected creatures inside the cone.

conjure-barrage::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The Dexterity save for half Force damage is represented, and structured damage was corrected to 5d8 Force.
conjure-barrage::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by correcting structured Damage Dice to 5d8 and Damage Type to Force.
conjure-barrage::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: No JSON save/damage change required beyond existing valid 5d8 Force save-half damage.

conjure-barrage::vision_light_sound.issue: The visible-creature requirement is represented by lineOfSight true after correction.
conjure-barrage::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by changing structured Line of Sight to true.
conjure-barrage::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing runtime targeting.lineOfSight to true.

conjure-barrage::ward_alarm_or_trigger.issue: The spectral weapons launch and disappear immediately; this is instantaneous damage presentation, not a ward, alarm, or delayed trigger.

conjure-barrage::deferred_descriptive_fl.issue: The spectral weapon/ammunition visual is descriptive presentation for the Force damage and remains deferred flavor.
conjure-barrage::deferred_descriptive_fl.recommendedTemplateChange: No mechanical field required for this visual presentation.
conjure-barrage::deferred_descriptive_fl.recommendedJsonChange: No runtime field required for this visual presentation.

counterspell::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: Counterspell requires a Constitution save by the target caster, and a failed save dissipates the spell with no effect; runtime stores this as prose-only utility with condition always.
counterspell::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add spell-interruption save fields for Constitution save and failed-save spell cancellation.
counterspell::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime interruptSpell data with saveType Constitution and failed-save cancellation.

counterspell::reaction_or_opportunity_re.issue: Counterspell is a reaction to seeing a creature within 60 feet casting a spell, and failed save wastes the action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast while preserving the spell slot; runtime does not expose the reaction trigger or action-waste/slot-preserve outcome.
counterspell::reaction_or_opportunity_re.recommendedTemplateChange: Add reaction spell-interrupt fields for casting trigger, wasted action kind, and slot-not-expended outcome.
counterspell::reaction_or_opportunity_re.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime reactionTrigger and interruptedCastOutcome data.

create-food-and-water::choice_or_mode.issue: The food looking like a food of the caster's choice is cosmetic; the nourishing food/water quantities are tracked under created-resource buckets.
create-food-and-water::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: No mechanical choice field required for food appearance.
create-food-and-water::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: No runtime mechanical field required for food appearance.

create-food-and-water::terrain_or_surfac.issue: The food and water appear on the ground or in containers within range; runtime targets ground only and does not type the container option.
create-food-and-water::terrain_or_surfac.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime placement data for ground or containers.

create-food-and-water::created_object_or.issue: The spell creates 45 pounds of nourishing food and 30 gallons of clean water, but runtime stores the created quantities and spoilage only in prose.
create-food-and-water::created_object_or.recommendedTemplateChange: Add created resource fields for food pounds, water gallons, food quality, water cleanliness, and spoilage time.

create-food-and-water::resource_or_consu.issue: Food and water quantities, nourishment/dehydration relevance, and food spoilage after 24 hours are resource mechanics not first-class in runtime JSON.
create-food-and-water::resource_or_consu.recommendedTemplateChange: Add consumable resource fields for nourishment, hydration, quantities, and spoilage.
create-food-and-water::resource_or_consu.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime consumableResource data for food/water and spoilage.

create-food-and-water::environmental_cha.issue: The spell changes world state by creating clean water and food at a location or in containers.
create-food-and-water::environmental_cha.recommendedTemplateChange: Add environmental creation fields for food/water placement.
create-food-and-water::environmental_cha.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime environmentalCreation data or link to createdResources.

crusaders-mantle::attack_or_save_modifie.issue: Crusader's Mantle is now represented as hit-triggered extra Radiant damage for weapon or Unarmed Strike hits while in the aura.
crusaders-mantle::attack_or_save_modifie.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by marking structured Effect Type as BUFF, DAMAGE.
crusaders-mantle::attack_or_save_modifie.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing the runtime trigger to on_attack_hit and condition to hit.

crusaders-mantle::social_or_knowledge_ef.issue: The detector matched allies, but ally inclusion is target/effect scope for the aura, not a social or knowledge effect.

daylight::target_filter_or_eligibility.issue: Daylight can target a point or an object that isn't worn or carried; runtime now includes point and objects, but the not-worn-or-carried object filter is not first-class.
daylight::target_filter_or_eligibility.recommendedTemplateChange: Add object eligibility fields for not worn/carried and mode-specific point versus object targeting.
daylight::target_filter_or_eligibility.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime objectEligibility data for unattended object mode.

daylight::vision_light_sound.issue: Bright and dim radii are now represented, but object-origin Emanation, opaque-cover blocking, and dispelling overlapping Darkness from level 3 or lower are still prose-only.
daylight::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add light source mode fields, opaque cover blocker fields, and darkness dispel interaction fields.
daylight::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime lightMode, lightBlocker, and dispelDarkness data.

dispel-magic::choice_or_mode.issue: Dispel Magic can target a creature, object, or magical effect; runtime now allows creatures and objects, but magical effects are not a valid target category.
dispel-magic::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add magical_effect as a target category or a spell-effect target family.
dispel-magic::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime target support for magical effects.

dispel-magic::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: For ongoing spells of level 4 or higher, Dispel Magic requires a spellcasting ability check against DC 10 plus spell level; runtime stores the check in prose only.
dispel-magic::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add ability-check resolution fields for spellcasting ability checks with formula DCs.
dispel-magic::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime dispelCheck data for spellcasting ability versus 10 + spell level.

dispel-magic::conditional_ending.issue: Ongoing spells end automatically up to the cast slot level and end on successful checks above that level; runtime has no typed dispel/ending data.
dispel-magic::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add dispel fields for auto-ended spell level threshold and checked spell level threshold.
dispel-magic::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime dispelMagic data for automatic and checked ending.

elemental-weapon::choice_or_mode.issue: The caster chooses Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder for the weapon's extra damage; runtime stores the choices as a slash-delimited damage type.
elemental-weapon::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime choice data for elemental damage type instead of slash-delimited string.

elemental-weapon::attack_or_save_modifie.issue: The extra damage is now hit-triggered, but the +1/+2/+3 attack-roll bonus and tiered damage scaling remain prose-only.
elemental-weapon::attack_or_save_modifie.recommendedTemplateChange: Add weapon attack augment fields for attack bonus and tiered extra damage.
elemental-weapon::attack_or_save_modifie.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime attackAugment data for magic weapon attack bonus and slot-tiered damage.

elemental-weapon::environmental_change.issue: The elemental damage type choice does not create an environmental change.
elemental-weapon::environmental_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under damage type choice and attack augment fields.
elemental-weapon::environmental_change.recommendedJsonChange: Track under damage type choice and attackAugment data.

enemies-abound::choice_or_mode.issue: Affected creatures must choose targets randomly from visible creatures in range of the attack, spell, or ability; runtime stores this targeting override in prose only.
enemies-abound::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add random target-selection fields for affected creature actions.

enemies-abound::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The initial Intelligence save is now represented, but the repeat save whenever the target takes damage remains prose-only.
enemies-abound::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime repeatSave trigger for on_damage_taken.

enemies-abound::reaction_or_opportunity_.issue: If an enemy provokes an Opportunity Attack from the affected creature, it must make that attack if able; runtime has no forced opportunity attack behavior.
enemies-abound::reaction_or_opportunity_.recommendedTemplateChange: Add reaction behavior fields for must_take_opportunity_attack_if_able.

enemies-abound::target_filter_or_eligibi.issue: Enemies Abound's immunity-to-Frightened automatic success rule is now encoded as a save auto-outcome override.
enemies-abound::target_filter_or_eligibi.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by Save Auto Outcome auto_success with condition immune_to_frightened.
enemies-abound::target_filter_or_eligibi.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by runtime condition.saveOutcomeOverrides carrying immune_to_frightened -> auto_success.

enemies-abound::repeat_save_or_recurring.closedReason: Enemies Abound now records the damage-triggered Intelligence repeat save as structured Recurring Mechanics and runtime recurringMechanics data.
enemies-abound::repeat_save_or_recurring.issue: The target can repeat the Intelligence save each time it takes damage, ending the effect on success; runtime has no damage-triggered repeat save.
enemies-abound::repeat_save_or_recurring.recommendedTemplateChange: Add repeat save trigger value for each_time_takes_damage.
enemies-abound::repeat_save_or_recurring.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime repeatSave data with timing on_damage_taken.

enemies-abound::vision_light_sound.issue: The affected creature regards all creatures it can see as enemies and randomly targets among creatures it can see; sight drives the behavior but is prose-only.
enemies-abound::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: Add sight-scoped enemy perception and target-randomization fields.
enemies-abound::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime perceivedEnemies and visibleTargetPool data.

enemies-abound::status_or_state_change.issue: The Frightened text is an immunity prerequisite for auto-success, not a condition the spell applies.

enemies-abound::conditional_ending.issue: The cited 'until the spell ends' phrase is ordinary duration scoping; the special ending is tracked under damage-triggered repeat save.
enemies-abound::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: No separate conditional-ending field required for this reviewed row.
enemies-abound::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: No separate conditional-ending JSON change required.

erupting-earth::choice_or_mode.issue: Choosing a ground point is ordinary area-origin targeting and is now represented by ground targeting.
erupting-earth::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by changing structured Valid Targets to ground.
erupting-earth::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing runtime validTargets to ground.

erupting-earth::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The Dexterity save for half Bludgeoning damage is represented.

erupting-earth::terrain_or_surface.issue: Difficult Terrain is now represented, but clearing each 5-foot-square portion by hand for at least 1 minute is not typed.
erupting-earth::terrain_or_surface.recommendedTemplateChange: Add terrain clearing fields for portion size, clearing method, and time per portion.
erupting-earth::terrain_or_surface.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime terrainClearing data for 5-foot-square portions and 1 minute by hand.

erupting-earth::vision_light_sound.issue: The visible ground point requirement is represented by lineOfSight true and ground targeting.

erupting-earth::environmental_change.issue: The ground becomes Difficult Terrain until cleared; terrain creation is represented, but the until-cleared world-state cleanup details are still incomplete.

fast-friends::choice_or_mode.issue: Choosing one target is ordinary target selection; target eligibility details are tracked separately.
fast-friends::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: No choice/mode field required beyond targeting.

fast-friends::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The initial Wisdom save and Charmed condition are represented, and inappropriate default repeat-save data was removed, but harm/conflict-triggered Wisdom save with advantage while fighting is not typed.
fast-friends::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add conditional repeat save fields for harmful/conflicting tasks and fighting advantage.

fast-friends::target_filter_or_eligibili.issue: The target must be a Humanoid that can see and hear the caster and understand them; Humanoid and line of sight are represented, but hearing and understanding are not first-class.
fast-friends::target_filter_or_eligibili.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by communication prerequisite propagation: structured markdown now records hearing, understanding, and sight requirements.
fast-friends::target_filter_or_eligibili.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by communication prerequisite propagation: targeting.filter.communicationPrerequisites now carries the matching runtime facts.
fast-friends::target_filter_or_eligibili.reviewerNote: Closed because this target-filter finding is fully represented by the new communication prerequisite fields.

fast-friends::vision_light_sound.issue: Fast Friends' target sight, hearing, and understanding prerequisites are represented by Target Can See/Hear/Understand Caster and mirrored in runtime targeting/effect communicationPrerequisites.
fast-friends::vision_light_sound.recommendedTemplateChange: No new template field needed; existing target communication prerequisite fields cover this eligibility mechanic.
fast-friends::vision_light_sound.recommendedJsonChange: Runtime targeting and effect target filters now require target sight, hearing, and understanding of the caster.

fast-friends::message_or_communication.issue: The target must understand the caster and receive requested services/activities; runtime has no communication prerequisite or task payload fields.

fast-friends::social_or_knowledge_effect.issue: The Charmed target undertakes requested services in a friendly manner, can receive new tasks, and later knows it was charmed; runtime only stores Charmed.
fast-friends::social_or_knowledge_effect.recommendedTemplateChange: Add social-control task fields and post-effect awareness fields.
fast-friends::social_or_knowledge_effect.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime socialControl and aftermathAwareness data.

fast-friends::status_or_state_change.issue: The Charmed condition is represented, and inappropriate default escape/repeat-save data was removed.
fast-friends::status_or_state_change.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional status field required for Charmed itself.
fast-friends::status_or_state_change.recommendedJsonChange: No additional status JSON change required for Charmed itself.

fast-friends::conditional_ending.issue: The spell ends if the requested activity would result in certain death; this ending condition is prose-only.
fast-friends::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add conditional-ending fields for certain-death task requests.
fast-friends::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime ending condition for certain_death_activity.
fast-friends::conditional_ending.closedReason: Closed by adding the certain_death_activity_requested conditional-ending trigger and recording Fast Friends' certain-death request ending in structured markdown and runtime JSON.

fast-friends::aftermath_or_memory.issue: When the spell ends, the creature knows it was Charmed by the caster; runtime has no aftermath awareness field.
fast-friends::aftermath_or_memory.recommendedTemplateChange: Add aftermath awareness fields for knows_charmed_by_caster.

aura-of-vitality.canonicalEvidence: When you create the aura and at the start of each of your turns while it persists, you can restore 2d6 Hit Points to one creature in it.
aura-of-vitality.structuredState: Effect Type: HEALING; Damage Dice: 2d6; Damage Type: Healing.
aura-of-vitality.jsonState: Runtime JSON now has immediate and turn_start HEALING effects for 2d6.
aura-of-vitality.issue: Aura of Vitality's recurring healing is now represented directly instead of as bonus-action utility prose.
aura-of-vitality.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional healing field required for this reviewed row.
aura-of-vitality.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding immediate and turn_start HEALING effects.

bestow-curse.canonicalEvidence: If you cast this spell using a level 4 spell slot, you can maintain Concentration on it for up to 10 minutes. If you use a level 5+ spell slot, the spell doesn't require Concentration, and the duration becomes 8 hours (level 5-6 slot) or 24 hours (level 7-8 slot). If you use a level 9 spell slot, the spell lasts until dispelled.
bestow-curse.structuredState: Scaling Rule 1 records the special higher-level prose.
bestow-curse.jsonState: Runtime scaling has no typed duration or concentration changes by slot level.
bestow-curse.issue: Closed: Bestow Curse's slot-tier duration and concentration changes are now structured and runtime-mapped.
bestow-curse.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by changing Scaling Rule 1 to slot_level_table for duration.concentration.
bestow-curse.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by runtime higherLevelScaling slot_level_table entries for level 4, levels 5-6, levels 7-8, and level 9.
bestow-curse.closedReason: Bestow Curse now records the higher-slot duration and concentration override table in structured markdown and runtime JSON.

blinding-smite.canonicalEvidence: Reaction Trigger: immediately after you hit a creature with a melee weapon or Unarmed Strike.
blinding-smite.structuredState: Reaction Trigger records the after-hit casting trigger.
blinding-smite.jsonState: Runtime JSON uses immediate damage/status triggers and does not expose the spell's casting trigger.
blinding-smite.issue: Blinding Smite is cast after a triggering melee weapon or unarmed hit, but runtime timing does not preserve that trigger.
blinding-smite.recommendedTemplateChange: Add casting trigger fields for after-hit smite-style spells, including allowed attack sources and target binding.
blinding-smite.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime castingTrigger data for immediately after hitting a creature with a melee weapon or Unarmed Strike.

call-lightning.canonicalEvidence: Until the spell ends, you can take a Magic action to call down lightning in that way again, targeting the same point or a different one.
call-lightning.structuredState: Effect Type: DAMAGE; no repeat action field.
call-lightning.jsonState: Runtime JSON has only an immediate damage effect.
call-lightning.issue: Call Lightning's repeat Magic action to call another bolt is not represented.
call-lightning.recommendedTemplateChange: Add sustain/reuse action fields for Magic action repeat strikes.
call-lightning.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime sustainAction/repeatEffect data for repeated lightning calls.

catnap.canonicalEvidence: up to three willing creatures of your choice that you can see within range
catnap.structuredState: Targeting now records multi, max 3, creatures.
catnap.jsonState: Runtime targeting now records multi, maxTargets 3, creatures, lineOfSight true, but no willing target filter.
catnap.issue: Catnap requires willing creature targets; willingness is not first-class in runtime targeting.
catnap.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by Target Willingness propagation: structured markdown now records Target Willingness for every spell and required for this direct willing-target spell.
catnap.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by Target Willingness propagation: targeting.filter.willing is required for this spell and not_applicable elsewhere.
catnap.reviewerNote: Closed because the new structured/runtime willingness field fully covers this direct willing-target mechanic.

catnap.canonicalEvidence: At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can target one additional willing creature for each slot level above 3rd.
catnap.structuredState: Scaling Rule 1 records special_text target scaling.
catnap.jsonState: Runtime targeting maxTargets is 3 with no target-count scaling data.
catnap.issue: Catnap's higher-level target count scaling is not represented in runtime data.
catnap.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime targetScaling data for +1 target per slot above 3.
catnap.closedReason: Catnap now records higher-slot target-count scaling with structured targeting.maxTargets scaling notes and runtime targeting.maxTargets slot-level thresholds.

conjure-animals.canonicalEvidence: Whenever the pack moves within 10 feet of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters a space within 10 feet of the pack or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 3d10 Slashing damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
conjure-animals.structuredState: Save Stat: Dexterity; Save Outcome currently says half, but canonical has no half-damage success branch.
conjure-animals.jsonState: Runtime has no damage effect for the 2024 pack trigger.
conjure-animals.issue: Conjure Animals needs a once-per-turn proximity save trigger with no half-damage success branch; current structured and runtime layers do not preserve it.
conjure-animals.recommendedTemplateChange: Add once-per-turn proximity trigger fields and correct the save outcome away from half when that field family exists.
conjure-animals.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime packDamageTrigger data for movement/entry/end-turn triggers, Dexterity save, and 3d10 Slashing on failed save only.
conjure-animals.closedReason: Conjure Animals now records the once-per-turn pack proximity Dexterity save and Slashing damage as structured Recurring Mechanics and runtime recurringMechanics data.

daylight.canonicalEvidence: For the duration, sunlight spreads from a point within range and fills a 60-foot-radius Sphere. Alternatively, you cast the spell on an object that isn't being worn or carried, causing the sunlight to fill a 60-foot Emanation originating from that object.
daylight.structuredState: Valid Targets: location_or_object; Area Shape: sphere.
daylight.jsonState: Runtime targeting now allows point and objects but has one Sphere area only.
daylight.issue: Daylight has point-origin Sphere and object-origin Emanation modes with different attachment behavior.
daylight.recommendedTemplateChange: Add light mode fields for point sphere versus object emanation.
daylight.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime lightModes data for point-origin sphere and object-origin emanation.

dispel-magic.canonicalEvidence: Choose one creature, object, or magical effect within range.
dispel-magic.jsonState: Runtime now allows creatures and objects, but not standalone magical effects.
dispel-magic.issue: Dispel Magic's magical-effect target type has no runtime target category.
dispel-magic.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime validTargets support for magical effects or a spellEffectTarget branch.

elemental-weapon.canonicalEvidence: A nonmagical weapon you touch becomes a magic weapon.
elemental-weapon.jsonState: Runtime targeting allows objects but has no nonmagical weapon filter.
elemental-weapon.issue: Elemental Weapon specifically requires a nonmagical weapon target.
elemental-weapon.recommendedTemplateChange: Add object subtype and nonmagical target filter fields.
elemental-weapon.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime objectFilter for weapon and nonmagical.

elemental-weapon.canonicalEvidence: A nonmagical weapon you touch becomes a magic weapon.
elemental-weapon.jsonState: Runtime has hit-triggered extra damage but no magic-weapon state.
elemental-weapon.issue: The target weapon becoming magical is a state change not represented in runtime data.
elemental-weapon.recommendedTemplateChange: Add object state fields for becomes_magic_weapon.
elemental-weapon.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime objectStateChange data for magic weapon.

erupting-earth.canonicalEvidence: A fountain of churned earth and stone erupts in a 20-foot cube centered on that point.
erupting-earth.jsonState: Runtime has DAMAGE and TERRAIN effects but no typed churned-earth creation beyond terrain.
erupting-earth.issue: The churned earth/stone is represented enough as Difficult Terrain for current runtime, but future terrain material fields could preserve material presentation if needed.
erupting-earth.recommendedTemplateChange: No new field required in this pass beyond terrain; optional future terrain material field could carry churned earth and stone.
erupting-earth.recommendedJsonChange: No runtime change required beyond the added TERRAIN effect.
